title: festuca ovina subsp. gracilior
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. gracilior, also known as fine fescue, is a type of grass that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of fine fescue include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress, and promote a good night's sleep. The herbal preparations of fine fescue can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
